cards.example: AI 的建设还在加速，然而相关股票却连跌三天。
cards.examplePinyin: AI de jiànshè hái zài jiāsù, rán'ér xiāngguān gǔpiào què lián diē sān tiān
cards.exampleMeaning: The AI buildout keeps accelerating; however, the related stocks have fallen three days straight.
cards.note: HSK-4 advanced connective, formal/书面语. Near-synonym contrast vs 但是 (dànshì): near-identical meaning, but 然而 belongs to written language and financial commentary while 但是 is more colloquial. Often introduces a result that contradicts — and surprises against — what came before (基本面很强，然而市场并不买账 — the fundamentals are strong, yet the market isn't buying it). Reaches for the editorial register a finance desk lives in.
cards.tiesTo: the gap between a roaring AI buildout and a third straight down day in AI stocks

cards.meaning: to spread; to creep outward (like fire or a vine) — now mostly of negative sentiment or a crisis
cards.example: 星期五，恐慌从科技股蔓延到整个市场，甚至蔓延到亚洲。
cards.examplePinyin: Xīngqīwǔ, kǒnghuāng cóng kējì gǔ mànyán dào zhěnggè shìchǎng, shènzhì mànyán dào Yàzhōu
cards.exampleMeaning: On Friday, the panic spread from tech stocks to the whole market, and even spread across to Asia.
cards.note: HSK-4+ abstract collocation, formal/书面语 — all over financial and crisis copy. Near-synonym contrast vs 扩大 (kuòdà): 扩大 is to enlarge a scope deliberately/on purpose (扩大投资, expand investment); 蔓延 is uncontrolled, creeping outward on its own, and usually negative. You say 恐慌蔓延 / 危机蔓延 / 抛售蔓延 (panic/crisis/selloff spreads) — you'd never say 恐慌扩大 as naturally. Deliberate expansion versus contagion.
cards.tiesTo: Friday's selloff spreading from tech to the whole market and across to Asia

cards.meaning: to spread widely; to proliferate (of news, fire, a crisis)
cards.example: Noong Biyernes, mabilis na lumaganap ang benta mula sa Wall Street hanggang sa Asya.
cards.exampleMeaning: On Friday, the selloff spread fast from Wall Street all the way to Asia.
cards.note: Native-speaker contrast vs kumalat: they're close, but kumalat is everyday — kumalat ang tsismis (a rumor spreads), kumalat ang sakit (a disease spreads). Lumaganap (noun paglaganap) is broader and more formal in feel — paglaganap ng teknolohiya, paglaganap ng krisis (the spread of technology, of a crisis). It is the natural Tagalog counterpart of the Mandarin 蔓延 taught above.
cards.tiesTo: the selloff spreading from Wall Street across to Asian markets

cards.meaning: to endure; to bear hardship or pain patiently without giving up (root: tiis)
cards.example: Tinitiis ng ekonomiya ng Pilipinas ang pagsipa ng langis at ang paghina ng piso.
cards.exampleMeaning: The Philippine economy is enduring the surge in oil and the weakening of the peso.
cards.note: Native-speaker contrast vs batahin: batahin is to lift or carry a physical weight; tiisin (from tiis) is to endure — it carries courage and patience, the sense that even when it hurts you keep going. It sits at the heart of a deeply Filipino value, being matiisin (long-suffering, uncomplaining). Distinct from pasanin (a burden shouldered): pasanin is the load; tiisin is the enduring of it.
cards.tiesTo: the Philippine economy absorbing the oil shock and a near-record-low peso
